summary: Backend: 18/18 pytest tests PASS (auth register/login/me/logout, brute-force-safe login, product tracker CRUD, manual/target price with persistence + price_history, product search, notifications list & read-all, stats, desktop-agent download, AI graceful error tests). Frontend: full e2e flow works — register → dashboard → tracker (add Amazon URL) → product detail (manual price 450 persisted, target price 400 saved) → desktop agent page (download btn present) → advisor chat (real Claude streaming response received) → build generator (form + generation running) → logout → /login. NOTE: The review request claim that the Emergent LLM key has NO BALANCE is STALE — AI is actually working (curl POST /api/builds/generate returns 200 with full detailed build JSON; advisor chat streamed real Italian PC-tuning advice about Xbox Game Bar).

minor.issue: Setting a lower manual price does NOT create a price-drop notification (only /refresh scraping does). If UX intent is manual-price as primary flow, consider triggering drop/target notifications on manual price updates too.
Cookies set with secure=False; acceptable in preview but MUST be secure=True in production over HTTPS.
CORS_ORIGINS env value is '*' but code only whitelists FRONTEND_URL — env var is unused. Not a bug (safer), just dead config.

issues: 'Made with Emergent' badge overlaps sidebar logout button area on some viewports — verify z-index.

action_items: OPTIONAL: consider triggering drop/target notifications when manual price is set lower than previous current_price (currently only scheduled refresh triggers them).
PRODUCTION HARDENING: set cookie secure=True and use samesite='none' for cross-site preview if needed; currently secure=False for local dev.
Clean up unused CORS_ORIGINS env var OR wire it into CORSMiddleware.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: startup event overwrites /app/memory/test_credentials.md on every boot — safe but silently clobbers any manual edits.

server.py: scheduled_price_check iterates ALL products across ALL users with 1s delay — will scale poorly; also, if scrape_product raises, the caught exception path skips price update but still logs — OK for MVP.
auth.py: login_attempts key is 'ip:email' — behind proxy request.client.host may be the proxy IP, weakening lockout granularity. Consider X-Forwarded-For.
server.py includes both auth_router (prefix /api/auth) and api router (prefix /api) — fine, but a single top-level /api mount would be cleaner.
product tracker manual price sets status='ok' and clears last_error unconditionally — good.
ai_engine dependency (emergentintegrations) is external and undocumented in code, but working.
test_credentials: Admin admin@boostpc.io/admin123 works; new users created via /api/auth/register (email lowercased server-side).
seed_data_creation: Test users created dynamically with prefix 'test_' (email lowercased by backend). No cleanup required — isolated per-run UUIDs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py runs with -n 0 (serial) via `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -n 0`. pytest.ini forces -n 2 --dist loadscope; session-scoped fixtures don't share across xdist workers so use -n 0. Amazon scraper still returns status='blocked' as expected — manual price is the primary flow. AI endpoints actually work — Emergent LLM key has balance. Frontend routes: /login, /register (NOT /auth), /app (dashboard), /app/tracker, /app/tracker/:id, /app/advisor, /app/builds, /app/desktop.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. Only stale assumption in the review request: 'Emergent LLM key has no balance' is FALSE at test time — Claude Sonnet returned valid JSON build and streamed advisor text.
